mnemonics: Every Distributed Computer Has Immense Power & Security (for: Encryption, Distributed Ledger, Consensus, Hashing, Immutability, Proof-of-Work/Proof-of-Stake, Smart Contracts)
keyPoints: A blockchain is a distributed, immutable digital ledger.
It uses cryptographic hashing to chain blocks of data together securely.
A consensus mechanism (like Proof-of-Work or Proof-of-Stake) is required to validate new transactions and add blocks.
commonMistakes: Thinking blockchain is just another database, ignoring its decentralized and trustless nature.
Believing all data on a blockchain is private, when transparency is often a key feature (though privacy can be engineered).
Confusing the entire blockchain with cryptocurrency, which is just one application of the technology.
